title: Mastering System Design
subtitle: From Foundations to Global Scale
author: Wordsus Team
description: Learn the core principles and advanced patterns for designing scalable, reliable, and maintainable distributed systems. This comprehensive guide covers everything from networking fundamentals and database sharding to consensus algorithms and real-world case studies.

chapters.title: Introduction to System Design
chapters.description: Introduction to what system design is, its lifecycle, metrics like latency and throughput, and engineering trade-offs.

chapters.title: Core Architectural Principles
chapters.description: Explore monolithic vs microservices architectures, separation of concerns, and the CAP and PACELC theorems.

chapters.title: Networking Foundations
chapters.description: Deep dive into OSI/TCP models, DNS, networking protocols (TCP/UDP), HTTP versions, and real-time communication.

chapters.title: API Design and Paradigms
chapters.description: Master REST, GraphQL, gRPC, API gateways, rate limiting, and versioning strategies.

chapters.title: Relational Databases and SQL
chapters.description: Advanced SQL, relational algebra, ACID properties, indexing with B-Trees, and query optimization.

chapters.title: NoSQL Databases and Non-Relational Stores
chapters.description: Study key-value, document, column-family, and graph databases and their use cases.

chapters.title: Caching Strategies
chapters.description: Implement caching layers, eviction policies (LRU/LFU), and strategies like write-through or write-back.

chapters.title: Data Partitioning and Sharding
chapters.description: Scale databases horizontally using partitioning criteria, sharding keys, and consistent hashing.

chapters.title: Replication and Consistency Models
chapters.description: Understand leader-based replication, consensus protocols, and strong vs eventual consistency models.

chapters.title: Load Balancing and Reverse Proxies
chapters.description: Master Layer 4/7 load balancing, algorithms like round-robin, and high availability with reverse proxies.

chapters.title: Asynchronous Processing and Message Queues
chapters.description: Build decoupled systems using message queues (RabbitMQ, Kafka), pub-sub, and background workers.

chapters.title: Distributed Search and Logging
chapters.description: Implement full-text search, centralized logging, distributed tracing, and monitoring/alerting systems.

chapters.title: Content Delivery Networks (CDNs)
chapters.description: Leverage edge computing, push/pull CDN models, geo-routing, and security at the edge.

chapters.title: Consensus Algorithms and Coordination
chapters.description: Solve the Byzantine Generals problem with Paxos, Raft, and distributed coordination services like Zookeeper.

chapters.title: Distributed Transactions
chapters.description: Manage data integrity across services using 2PC, 3PC, the Saga pattern, and compensating transactions.

chapters.title: Fault Tolerance and Chaos Engineering
chapters.description: Build resilient systems with circuit breakers, bulkhead patterns, retries, and chaos engineering practices.

chapters.title: Security in Distributed Systems
chapters.description: Implement IAM, OAuth 2.0, mTLS, and defense mechanisms against DDoS and data breaches.

chapters.title: Designing Social Media Feeds and Timelines
chapters.description: Case study on building scalable feeds, handling fan-out problems, and optimizing media storage.

chapters.title: Designing Video Streaming Platforms
chapters.description: Case study on video encoding, adaptive bitrate streaming, and distributed delivery at scale.

chapters.title: Designing Location-Based Services
chapters.description: Case study on spatial data indexing, quadtrees, real-time tracking, and ride-hailing architectures.

chapters.title: Designing E-commerce Platforms
chapters.description: Case study on product catalogs, inventory management, payment gateways, and handling flash sales.

parts.title: Part I: Foundations of System Design
Part II: Data Storage and Management
Part IV: Advanced Distributed Systems Concepts
Part V: Real-World Case Studies and Patterns
